title: World’s Gone Mad: ‘Left-wing loonies’ rush to Cuba to prevent its communist regime
description: Sky News host Rowan Dean says “left-wing loonies” are rushing off to Cuba to prevent the collapse of its communist regime.

title: ‘Tragic and dramatic’: Lefty has a meltdown after ICE agents takeover US airports
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio comments on the left-wing reaction to ICE agents restoring order at American airports.    “The democrats claim ICE agents will kill people in airports, and the left are freaking out,” Ms De Giorgio said.   “Why are they just so tragic and dramatic?”

title: Trump roasts Australia for not giving the US support
description: Former speaker of the house Bronwyn Bishop believes US President Donald Trump is right to criticise Prime Minister Anthony Albanese for not giving the US support.  “He remembers when he asked for a ship before, and we said no,” Ms Bishop told Sky News host Rowan Dean.  “We had Albanese saying today we’ve never said no when we’ve been asked for help, he just can’t help himself to tell lies.  “The honest truth is, we haven’t got much we can send.”

title: ‘The worst political hyperbole’: Democrats claim ICE will kill Americans in airports
description: Fox News contributor Paul Mauro believes the Democrats want to rig the system to allow illegal immigrants to vote ahead in the US midterms.   “Democrats claim that ICE will be willy nilly pulling people out of airport lines … and potentially killing Americans, is the worst kind of political hyperbole,” Mr Mauro told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.   “What the Democrats need to do here is to continue to play the ice card and continue to deform that narrative for their purposes.  “ICE is not out there hunting people … and killing them randomly.    “There are laws on the books for America to remove illegal immigrants.”

title: ‘Rotten’ journalists' pay exposed after going on strike
description: Former Labor MP Michael Danby exposes ABC journalists' pay after they go on strike, asking for a raise.   “I think Andrew revealed that the average wage there is $112,000, which is extraordinary,” Mr Danby told Sky News host Rowan Dean.  “If there is ever a non-socialist left government elected in Canberra, especially in the Senate, they must force the ABC to publish the table because that’s the first step to accountability in that rotten institution.”

title: World’s Gone Mad: Hollywood riddled with ‘woke garbage’
description: Sky News host Rowan Dean reacts to “woke garbage” in Hollywood films with diversity standards.

title: PM acting ‘slippery’ saying Australia never received a formal request from US to help with war
description: Strategic Analysis Australia Director Michael Shoebridge claims Americans informally discussed with Australia about sending aid to the Middle East.  “The Prime Minister and our Deputy Prime Minister, who I think is also still the Defence Minister, are being slippery here,” Mr Shoebridge told Sky News host Caleb Bond.  “The Americans informally sound out a partner like Australia about whether we would be willing to contribute, and if so, what.  “Only after those informal discussions do we get a request.  “The Americans have sounded out our officials and our government, and they’ve been politely told, well, not only do we not really want to contribute anything, but our cupboard is bare because our military has been mismanaged.   “There hasn’t been a formal request because they know it would get a big fat no.”

title: One Nation ‘needs’ to be in Coalition for it to be ‘glorious’
description: Sky News host Rowan Dean would like to see a “glorious” Coalition.   “Pauline Hanson has said, she said that she would be very happy to use her numbers to help elect a coalition government,” Mr Dean said.   “One Nation needs to be inside the tent.”

title: Cost of living forces younger voters to a ‘political reckoning’ with One Nation
description: Sky News contributor Louise Roberts explains why One Nation is gaining younger voters’ support.   “This is because the defining experience of millennial adulthood hasn’t been activism or ideology, but cost of living pain,” Ms Roberts told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “That’s where One Nation enters a frame, occupying a space which really should be Coalition territory.  “It turns out millennials are much more like the rest of us.  “They are voters under pressure.”

title: Millennials ‘shifting away’ from Labor as One Nation gains seven point rise in two weeks
description: Sky News contributor Louise Roberts reveals the results of a Sky News Pulse YouGov poll.  “30 per cent of millennials now say they would place One Nation first on their ballot,” Ms Roberts told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “That figure represents a seven-point rise in just two weeks.  “It is a major shift away from traditional Labor support, with Labor now ranking second amongst millennials in the same poll.”

title: One Nation’s polling numbers should be ‘setting off alarm bells’ for Labor Party
description: Sky News contributor Louise Roberts reveals the Labor Party’s biggest threat.   “There is a telling shift happening in Australian politics, and it is coming from a group long assumed to safely be in Labor’s corner. I’m talking about millennials,” Ms Roberts told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.   “Roughly 30-45 years of age who are drifting towards Pauline Hanson’s One Nation in numbers that should be setting off alarm bells across Canberra.”

title: Donald Trump sees Keir Starmer as ‘weak and indecisive’
description: Sky News contributor Adrian Hayes says US President Donald Trump doesn’t think “too highly” of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer.  Mr Hayes told Sky News host Steve Price that Donald Trump sees Keir Starmer as “weak and indecisive”.  “And of course, with a character like the US President, he’s going to pick on this.”

title: ‘Out of control’ public spending has left the UK Defence Force without funding
description: Sky News contributor Adrian Hayes says the UK can’t spend more on defence with “out of control” public spending.  Mr Hayes told Sky News host Steve Price the UK’s debt is 2.8 trillion pounds.  “You’ve got to cull public spending before you can start spending more money on defence.”

title: Public support for Iran war is ‘waning’
description: Sky News contributor Adrian Hayes says public support for the Iran war is “waning”.  Mr Hayes Iran “deserved the bloody noise” for its terrible regime.  “But there are impacts and tradeoffs.”

title: ‘Wake-up call’: UK public spending in the spotlight after Iran war began
description: Sky News contributor Adrian Hayes says the UK has had a “wake-up call” on its spending habits after the Iran war began.  Mr Hayes told Sky News host Steve Price it will take “many years” for the UK to recover financially.  “You can’t cut public spending unless you cull the size of the state.”
